mnemonics: CIS: Construct Injection, find Set where images are equal.
One-to-One Tickets: If you have an injection to the show and from the show, you can get a bijection.
keyPoints: If there exist injective functions f: A -> B and g: B -> A, then there exists a bijective function h: A -> B.
The proof constructs the bijection by partitioning the sets into chains using the concept of 'ancestors'.
It is a fundamental theorem for comparing cardinalities of sets without explicitly constructing a bijection.
commonMistakes: Assuming the injections f and g themselves are the bijection; they are only used to construct it.
Incorrectly applying the theorem to infinite sets by trying to 'count' elements, rather than relying on the structural property of injections.
Forgetting that the theorem requires both injections to exist; one injection alone is not sufficient.
